benchmark_details.name: HaluBench
benchmark_details.overview: HaluBench is a comprehensive hallucination evaluation benchmark, consisting of 15k samples sourced from various real-world domains. It evaluates the hallucination detection capabilities of language models.

purpose_and_intended_users.goal: To provide a reference-free automated evaluation methodology for hallucination detection in retrieval-augmented generation systems.

data.source: Various existing Question Answering datasets including CovidQA, PubmedQA, DROP, and FinanceBench.
data.annotation: Manual annotation for hallucination detection based on contextual consistency.

methodology.calculation: Accuracy is calculated based on the proportion of correct detections of hallucinations.
methodology.interpretation: Higher accuracy indicates better performance in detecting hallucinations.
methodology.validation: Validation was performed with human annotations confirming high-quality data and label agreement.
